summary: Implemented ProgressiveOverload service with calculateSuggestion, calculateSuggestionForWorkoutExercise, recordResult, and recalculateChain methods. Calculates weight recommendations based on historical set performance with increase/maintain/decrease directions, consecutive count tracking, and plate-rounding for decrease values.
keyDecisions: 2.1: Consecutive counts computed on-the-fly from WorkoutSet history rather than stored, making recalculateChain a no-op
2.1: OverloadSuggestion.suggestedWeight changed from number to number|null to support no-history case
2.1: calculateSuggestion operates per exercise_biz_key; calculateSuggestionForWorkoutExercise operates per WorkoutExercise.biz_key for US-17
2.1: Skipped exercises (exercise_status=skipped) and in_progress session exercises excluded from overload calculation
2.1: Decrease weight rounded to nearest increment multiple via Math.round(value/step)*step
2.1: recordResult and recalculateChain are no-ops since state is computed from DB on-the-fly; future extensions can add caching

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: All sets met → direction='increase', suggestedWeight = previousWeight + exercise.increment
Some sets missed → direction='maintain', suggestedWeight = previousWeight
Consecutive 2 sessions missed → direction='decrease', suggestedWeight = previousWeight * 0.9
Consecutive 3 sessions completed → UI hint '考虑加大增量？' (no auto-modify)
No history → suggestedWeight = null, reason explains first-time input needed
Decrease value rounded to nearest plate combination via roundToNearest
recalculateChain(exerciseBizKey, fromDate) rebuilds suggestion chain from a given date
Unit tests: 90%+ coverage for all overload scenarios

notes: 25 dedicated progressive-overload tests covering all acceptance criteria including US-10 (in_progress exclusion), US-16 (skipped exclusion), US-17 (independent per-WorkoutExercise chains). Overall project coverage 93.48%, progressive-overload.ts at 93.9% statements.
